label: fiction
title: The Nine-Minute Gap
charge: Causing death by dangerous driving, and failing to stop after a collision
elements: The car that struck the cyclist was the accused's.
He failed to stop and report the collision.

beats.text: The accused's wife testifies he was home by half past eleven; they watched the late news together. She remembers because the lead story annoyed him.
beats.reveal_note: An alibi of habit, told in loyalty or in good faith. The car's own record puts its bonnet into a cyclist eleven minutes after the news began.

beats.text: The body-shop manager describes the bonnet damage he repaired that week: 'Looked like a deer strike — we see them all the time on that road.'
beats.reveal_note: The damage height matches handlebars, not a deer's body line, and no hair or hide was found in the repair. 'We see them all the time' is a story about other cars.

beats.text: A collision investigator extracts the car's airbag control module: a recorded frontal impact at 23:41 that night, speed logged at 47 in a 30 zone. The system noted the event; the airbags did not deploy.
beats.reveal_note: The car kept the diary its driver wouldn't. An impact at 23:41, at 47 in a 30, is the collision — timed and measured.

beats.text: Paint analysis: flakes recovered from the cyclist's frame are a two-stage respray matching the batch used on the accused's car at its last repaint.
beats.reveal_note: Batch-level paint transfer ties this car — not a car like it — to the bicycle.

beats.text: The judge on circumstantial evidence: strands of a cord, not links of a chain — no single strand need bear the whole weight, but together they may hold it.
beats.reveal_note: Module, paint, and the nine unexplained minutes plait together; the alibi is the strand that snaps.

twist: The phone stayed home and the wife stayed loyal, but the car testified: a logged impact at 23:41, forty-seven in a thirty, and its respray batch on the cyclist's frame. The 'deer strike' stood at handlebar height.
